examples.title: Algorithm: Producer-Consumer
examples.description: Demonstrates synchronization between producers and consumers using semaphores
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the producer-consumer problem, where producers add items to a buffer and consumers remove items from the buffer. The semaphores mutex, empty, and full are used to synchronize access to the buffer.

examples.title: Python: Producer-Consumer
examples.description: Implementation of the producer-consumer problem using Python and semaphores
examples.code:
import threading
import time

# Initialize semaphores
mutex = threading.Semaphore(1)
empty = threading.Semaphore(10)
full = threading.Semaphore(0)

# Initialize buffer
buffer = []

# Producer function
def producer():
 for i in range(10):
 # Produce an item
 time.sleep(1)
 empty.acquire()
 mutex.acquire()
 # Add item to buffer
 buffer.append(i)
 mutex.release()
 full.release()

# Consumer function
def consumer():
 for i in range(10):
 full.acquire()
 mutex.acquire()
 # Remove item from buffer
 item = buffer.pop(0)
 mutex.release()
 empty.release()
 # Consume the item
 print(item)
 time.sleep(1)

# Create threads
producer_thread = threading.Thread(target=producer)
consumer_thread = threading.Thread(target=consumer)

# Start threads
producer_thread.start()
consumer_thread.start()

# Wait for threads to finish
producer_thread.join()
consumer_thread.join()
examples.explanation: This Python code implements the producer-consumer problem using semaphores. The producer function produces items and adds them to the buffer, while the consumer function removes items from the buffer and consumes them.

examples.title: C: Dining Philosophers
examples.description: Implementation of the dining philosophers problem using C and semaphores
examples.explanation: This C code implements the dining philosophers problem using semaphores. The philosopher function represents a philosopher who picks up two chopsticks, eats, and then puts down the chopsticks.
